items.title: The length of historical path and the digitalization speed of record labels: A path dependence perspective
items.author: Ryan W. Tang (Griffith Business School) & Yi Wang (University of Southern Denmark)
items.venue: Journal of Business Research, Vol. 189, Article 115150 (Elsevier, peer-reviewed; available online 18 December 2024)
items.verbatim_quote: In comparison with traditional formats of music media such as vinyl, cassette, and compact disc (CD), digitalization requires that music organizations replace the skills and knowledge accumulated throughout their historical paths with new capabilities suited for the digital environment.
items.why_relevant: Empirically grounds (5,129 labels, 68 countries, 2000–2018) why long-tenured labels are slow to digitize their catalogs — directly justifies Sharon's catalog-management dashboard as a capability-replacement tool, not just a UI.

items.title: Metrics and decision-making in music streaming
items.author: Arnt Maasø & Anja Nylund Hagen (University of Oslo)
items.venue: Popular Communication: The International Journal of Media and Culture, Vol. 18, Issue 1 (Special Issue: The Spotification of Popular Culture), pp. 18–31 (Taylor & Francis, peer-reviewed)
items.verbatim_quote: My job is about getting up in the morning, and then I log on to see the analytics [...]. It's like a speedometer that we didn't have before the streaming economy, and it's fantastic. That's the key, really, even in studio work and A&R-ing.
items.why_relevant: Peer-reviewed interview data showing managers and label execs treat analytics dashboards as their daily 'speedometer' — validates positioning Sharon's panel as a first-thing-in-the-morning monitoring surface, not a back-office report.

items.title: Unclaimed Royalties: Best Practice Recommendations for the Mechanical Licensing Collective — A Report of the Register of Copyrights
items.author: U.S. Copyright Office (Shira Perlmutter, Register of Copyrights; Office of the General Counsel)
items.venue: U.S. Copyright Office, July 2021 (statutory report under the Orrin G. Hatch–Bob Goodlatte Music Modernization Act, Pub. L. No. 115-264)
items.verbatim_quote: The MMA represents a new era for music licensing, providing an opportunity for increased transparency to musical work copyright owners and songwriters regarding the compulsory licensing of their works. The establishment of the MLC as a central collective to administer the new blanket license, along with other changes—such as expanded DMP reporting requirements, the creation of a public musical works database, and the ability to search through and claim unmatched usage—provide a strong foundation for working toward the overall goal of reducing the incidence of unclaimed royalties.
items.why_relevant: Federal-government source establishing the MLC + public musical-works database as the canonical rights-tracking infrastructure — anchors why Sharon's catalog dashboard must surface match status, ownership shares, and unclaimed-royalty visibility.
